title: Effects of the Minimum Wage on Employment of Young Adults With Cognitive Disabilities
authors: Barry R. Chiswick; Hope Corman; Dhaval Dave; Nancy E. Reichman
abstract: ABSTRACT We estimate the effects of increases in the minimum wage on labor market outcomes of young adults (ages 21–49) with cognitive disabilities, a vulnerable and low‐skilled group, using data from the American Community Survey (2008–2023) and a generalized difference‐in‐differences research design. We found that a higher effective minimum wage reduces employment and labor force participation but has inconclusive effects on labor supply at the intensive margin for this group. Effects were stronger for those with lower educational attainment. We found no significant labor market effects of an increase in the minimum wage for individuals with ambulatory/sensory disabilities or in the non‐disabled population.
